--- SOURCE 1: Mars Habitability: A Planetary Science Perspective - Number Analytics ---
URL: https://www.numberanalytics.com/blog/mars-habitability-planetary-science

SUMMARY:
In situ analysis: Future Mars missions will include advanced instruments capable of in situ analysis, such as the Mars Sample Analysis (MSA) instrument . Orbital missions: Future orbital missions will provide high-resolution data on the Martian surface and atmosphere, which will help to identify potential biosignatures.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Mars exploration has included missions like Viking, Curiosity, and Perseverance, each contributing to our understanding of Martian habitability and the search for life.
Current missions prioritize identifying habitability indicators and biosignatures, such as organic molecules, crucial for understanding potential past life on Mars.
Advanced instruments, like the Mars Sample Analysis (MSA), enable in situ analysis, allowing direct examination of Martian soil and rock for biosignatures.
